page: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a virtual private server belonging to the High Performance family. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor clocked at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, but its multi-core CPU performance falls into the average tier. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; while large-block read and write bandwidths are average, small-block cached read bandwidth and overall PassMark memory scores are weak. Database operations and static web serving throughput are poor, ranking in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Due to its shared CPU allocation and limited 2.0 GB memory capacity, this server is positioned as a budget-friendly option for workloads with low resource density. The tradeoff of shared CPU resources and weak database performance makes it less suitable for production databases or high-throughput web serving. However, its strong single-core performance and integrated NVMe storage make it appropriate for lightweight background tasks, single-threaded utility scripts, or basic development environments.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server within the High Performance family, configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While stress-ng benchmarks place its single-core CPU performance in the top 10%, its multi-core performance is average, and its memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are weak. Furthermore, database operations and static web serving throughput fall into the bottom 10%. The shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity represent a clear tradeoff, limiting its utility for high-throughput production databases. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads, basic development environments, and single-threaded utility tasks where cost efficiency and high single-core burst capability are prioritized over sustained multi-core throughput or intensive memory operations.
og_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 60 GB of NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside weak memory and database metrics.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is an x86_64 server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 60 GB NVMe storage, suited for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared AMD EPYC virtual server with strong single-core performance and local NVMe storage for lightweight development workloads.

2.0 GB RAM and 60 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests
Weak memory bandwidth and poor database operation benchmarks
